title: hieracium olivaceum subsp. subluridum
intro: Hieracium olivaceum subsp. subluridum, also known as a subspecies of the lesser hawkbit, is a perennial plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and native to certain regions of Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating gastrointestinal issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Hieracium olivaceum subsp. subluridum include its potential to aid digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
